response: We propose a novel method for [task/domain], evaluated against a strong baseline on the standard test set. Across five random seeds, our method achieves 73.4 ± 1.1% accuracy, compared to 72.2 ± 0.9% for the baseline, corresponding to a mean absolute improvement of +1.2 percentage points. A two-sample t-test yields t = 1.04, p = 0.31, indicating that the difference is not statistically significant (n.s.), with a 95% confidence interval for the gain of [–1.1, +3.5]. These results suggest that our approach performs at least as well as the baseline under these experimental conditions, though the observed improvement may be due to chance. Our analysis underscores the importance of rigorous statistical testing and adequate sample sizes when benchmarking incremental advances, and provides an empirical reference point for future research on this task.
